title: Sequence Labeling Applications
concept: Sequence labeling powers information extraction, grammatical correction, and keyword extraction systems. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Language Modeling.'

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of Sequence Labeling?

options.text: Assigning a tag or label to individual words in a sentence
Sorting your emails alphabetically by the sender's name
Generating a completely new image from a text description

exercises.explain: Sequence labeling looks at a sequence of words and assigns a label to each word in that sequence.

cards.title: Text with Superpowers
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how your phone automatically highlights phone numbers, dates, and addresses inside a text message so you can click them? It isn't magic—it is a smart AI technique called Sequence Labeling!

cards.title: What is Sequence Labeling?
cards.body: Sequence labeling is a machine learning technique where an AI model assigns a label or tag to every single word (or 'token') in a sentence. Instead of classifying a whole document, it makes a decision for each individual word, identifying names, verbs, products, or key phrases as it reads.

cards.title: The Recipe Highlighter
cards.body: Imagine reading a cookbook and using a highlighter. You highlight ingredients in yellow, actions (like 'bake' or 'whisk') in pink, and cooking times in blue. Instead of summarizing the whole recipe, you are tagging individual words based on their role. Sequence labeling does the exact same thing with digital text!

cards.title: Calendar Auto-Fill
cards.body: Think of email programs scanning messages. If you receive an email saying 'Let us meet at Starbucks on Friday,' a sequence labeling model marks 'Starbucks' as a LOCATION and 'Friday' as a DATE. This allows your app to suggest adding the event to your calendar automatically!

cards.title: Not Just for Grammar Class
cards.body: Many people think sequence labeling is only used for grammar class tasks like identifying nouns and verbs. In reality, it runs the search engines, email assistants, and automatic translation systems you use every single day!

cards.title: Word-by-Word vs. Whole Text
cards.body: Let's look at how sequence labeling differs from standard text classification. Notice how classification looks at the whole picture, while sequence labeling looks at each piece.

right.text: Labels the entire sentence with one category: 'Travel Review' [CLASS].

exercises.prompt: Which of the following apps relies on sequence labeling to highlight grammar mistakes in your essays?

options.text: An AI writing assistant like Grammarly

exercises.explain: Grammatical error correction systems use sequence labeling to tag each word as 'correct', 'delete', 'replace', or 'insert' to fix writing.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Sequence labeling assigns a single tag to an entire document rather than tagging individual words.
exercises.explain: Sequence labeling works on the level of individual words (tokens), labeling them one by one rather than giving one single label to the entire text.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete how sequence labeling operates on text.
exercises.sentence: A sequence labeling system will look at every word and assign a specific label to it.
exercises.explain: Sequence labeling models examine each word individually and assign it a specific label (or tag) based on its role in the sentence.

exercises.prompt: Which of these AI systems is actively performing sequence labeling?

options.text: System A: It reads a product review and decides if it is overall positive or negative.
System B: It reads a recipe and highlights every ingredient name and every measurement unit.
System C: It writes a brand new recipe based on a list of ingredients you have in your fridge.

exercises.explain: System B is the only sequence labeling system because it examines individual words and tags them directly. System A does classification, and System C does generation.

exercises.prompt: You want to build a system that automatically finds and labels all product names in thousands of user reviews. How does a sequence labeling system solve this?

options.text: It predicts a single category tag for a whole paragraph.
It scans the text word-by-word, assigning a tag (like 'Product' or 'Verb') to each token.
It translates the text from English to Spanish.
It scrambles the order of words to test reading comprehension.

exercises.explain: Fantastic job! Sequence labeling isn't just about parsing grammar; it's the engine behind extractors, correctors, and tagging systems everywhere. Next up: Language Modeling.
